title: subworkflow - github operations handover update remediation runbook
description: GitHub remediation/orchestration subworkflow for operations-handover.\n\nThis workflow writes or updates an operational control document in GitHub and enforces non-fire-and-forget behavior using explicit wait and verification tasks.
guide:
## 🧩 **Subworkflow - github operations handover update remediation runbook**

📦 This `github` subworkflow template is provided to support reusable workflow automation patterns.

It should be adapted, validated, and governed based on your environment, permissions, and operational standards.

---

### 🎯 **Use Case**

- GitHub remediation/orchestration subworkflow for operations-handover. This workflow writes or updates an operational control document in GitHub and enforces non-fire-and-forget behavior using explicit wait and verification tasks.
- Supports workflow-as-code practices and modular orchestration.
- Designed to be called by parent workflows or run as a reusable building block.

---

### ⚙️ **Parameters**

- **githubOwner** (Template input): Set according to your environment. Default example: `""`
- **githubRepository** (Template input): Set according to your environment. Default example: `""`
- **githubBranch** (Template input): Set according to your environment. Default example: `"main"`
- **githubConnectionId** (Template input): Set according to your environment. Default example: `""`
- **createNewBranch** (Template input): Set according to your environment. Default example: `"false"`
- **commitMessage** (Template input): Set according to your environment. Default example: `"chore(automation): update-remediation-runbook for operations-handover"`
- **filePathOverride** (Template input): Set according to your environment. Default example: `""`
- **fileContentOverride** (Template input): Set according to your environment. Default example: `""`
- **primaryResourceId** (Template input): Set according to your environment. Default example: `""`
- **incidentId** (Template input): Set according to your environment. Default example: `""`
- **serviceName** (Template input): Set according to your environment. Default example: `""`

---

### 🧠 **Workflow Logic**

- Execute the configured task chain in sequence based on predecessor and condition settings.
- Task step: `github_create_or_replace_file_1`
- Task step: `wait-for-github-operation`
- Task step: `check-status`
- If any critical action fails, the subworkflow fails so parent automation can retry or branch safely.

---

### ✅ **Validation Before Production**

- Test this subworkflow in non-production environments before deployment.
- Validate input values, task conditions, retries, timeout behavior, and connector permissions.
- Confirm error handling and rollback behavior meets your workflow automation standards.

---

### 🧩 **Usage Context**

- Fits into end-to-end automation and remediation workflows as a modular subworkflow.
- Can be versioned and promoted through CI/CD using workflow-as-code practices.
- Usage is at your own risk; ensure governance and approval gates are in place before production use.

github_create_or_replace_file_1.name: github_create_or_replace_file_1

input.fileContent:
{{ input()["fileContentOverride"] if input()["fileContentOverride"] else "# Automation Evidence

- Context: operations-handover
- Pattern: update-remediation-runbook
- Purpose: Update remediation runbook with latest outage handling steps.
- Source: Dynatrace workflow automation
- Status: generated
" }}

github_create_or_replace_file_1.description: Create or update GitHub file for orchestration evidence.

wait-for-github-operation.name: wait-for-github-operation
wait-for-github-operation.description: Wait for GitHub operation to expose commit/content SHA.

check-status.name: check-status
check-status.description: Final verification of GitHub operation success.
